description: Tool register for the gateway — built-in demo scripts.

tools.name: Echo
tools.summary: Return the provided arguments back unchanged.
tools.instructions: Echoes its structured args back in the result. Pass any JSON value as args and the same value is returned. Useful as a connectivity / round-trip smoke test.

tools.name: Base64 Encode/Decode
tools.summary: Encode text to Base64 or decode Base64 back to text.
tools.instructions: Args: { op: "encode"|"decode", text: string }. Both args required. encode -> Base64 of the UTF-8 text; decode -> UTF-8 from valid Base64 (malformed input is reported, not mangled).

tools.name: Hash
tools.summary: Compute a cryptographic digest (e.g. sha256) of input text.
tools.instructions: Args: { algo?: "sha256"|"sha1"|"md5" (default "sha256"), text: string }. Returns the lowercase hex digest of the UTF-8 text.

tools.name: UUID Generator
tools.summary: Generate a random RFC 4122 v4 UUID.
tools.instructions: Args: { count?: number (default 1) }. Returns one or more freshly generated v4 UUID strings.

tools.name: JSON Format
tools.summary: Validate and pretty-print a JSON string.
tools.instructions: Args: { text: string, indent?: number (default 2) }. Parses the input; returns the re-serialised, indented JSON, or a clear parse error.

tools.name: Text Stats
tools.summary: Count characters, words, and lines in a block of text.
tools.instructions: Args: { text: string }. Returns { chars, words, lines } computed from the input text.

tools.name: Danger Demo
tools.summary: Demonstrate a gated, potentially destructive operation.
tools.instructions: A sample tool that represents a destructive action. It is intended to be exercised behind the gate so the gating path can be tested end to end. Pass { confirm: true } to proceed.

tools.name: tf_tool_add
tools.summary: Register a new tool entry in the gateway tool register.
tools.instructions: Add a register entry. Args: { id, name, summary?, category?, instructions?, inputSchema?, invoke:{type:'script',path:'scripts/<file>'}|{type:'shell',command}, scriptText? }. inputSchema is a JSON Schema object for the tool's args - a HOT-promoted tool advertises it verbatim in the top-level tools/list, so author it if the tool may be promoted. If scriptText is given for a script invoke, the body is written to tools/scripts/<basename> first. Change is LIVE (no reconnect). Fails if id already exists.

tools.name: tf_tool_set
tools.summary: Activate, deactivate, promote (hot), hide, or remove a register tool.
tools.instructions: Args: { id, action:'enable'|'disable'|'hot'|'unhot'|'hide'|'unhide'|'remove' }. enable/disable flips the active overlay (default ON) — disabled tools drop from toolfunnel_list_tools. hot/unhot promote/demote on the TOP-LEVEL every-turn surface (tools/list), making it directly callable (default OFF). hide/unhide declutter the MANAGER views only (tf_list + UI) and do NOT change what the AI sees. The id may be a local tool id, an upstream surfaced name, or a meta-tool name. remove deletes the register entry and purges its state key. All LIVE (no restart).

tools.name: tf_mcp_add
tools.summary: Register an upstream MCP (and optionally expose some of its tools).
tools.instructions: Args: { id, command, args?, env?, transport?='stdio', enabled?=true, description?, expose?:[{tool, as?, category?, enabled?}] }. Adds an upstreams[] entry; each expose[] item adds a curated-direct selection. Curated-direct changes need an aggregator reconnect to surface.

tools.name: tf_mcp_set
tools.summary: Enable, disable, or remove an upstream MCP.
tools.instructions: Args: { id, action:'enable'|'disable'|'remove' }. enable/disable toggles the upstream; remove deletes it and cascades its expose entries. Reconnect needed for the curated-direct surface to change.

tools.name: tf_hook_add
tools.summary: Add a hook manifest entry (e.g. a PreToolUse gate).
tools.instructions: Args: { id, event, matcher?, command, script?, timeout?, enabled?=true, description?, scriptText? }. id format '<kebab-event>/<name>'. command should use the portable ${HOOKS_DIR} token. If scriptText is given, the script is authored under hooks/scripts/ (path-guarded). enabled is the manifest seed; live toggles live in hooks.state.json.

tools.name: tf_hook_set
tools.summary: Enable, disable, or remove a hook.
tools.instructions: Args: { id, action:'enable'|'disable'|'remove' }. enable/disable writes the hooks.state.json overlay (wins over the manifest seed) and mirrors the manifest; remove deletes the manifest entry and purges the overlay key (the script file is left in place).

tools.name: tf_list
tools.summary: List register tools, upstream MCPs, or hooks with their active state.
tools.instructions: Args: { kind:'tools'|'mcps'|'hooks' }. tools → register briefs + enabled/hidden overlay; mcps → upstreams + expose selections; hooks → configured + auto-detected inventory with live enabled state. Read-only.

tools.name: tf_log
tools.summary: Enable, disable, or check the gateway activity/audit log.
tools.instructions: Args: { action:'enable'|'disable'|'status', path? }. Toggles ToolFunnel's JSONL audit log (tool runs + gate allow/deny decisions). Default OFF. 'status' returns {enabled,path} + recent count. Like any tool, tf_log can itself be disabled to revoke logging control.

tools.name: tf_pack
tools.summary: Package the LIVE setup into a deployment-ready artifact under <home>/dist/ (portable config home, or a publishable npm package wrapping toolfunnel).
tools.instructions: Args: { format?:'home'|'npm' (default 'home'), name?, version?, description?, out?, force? }. Snapshots the current setup - tools + scripts, hot/hidden curation (tools.state.json), upstream references (mcp/expose.json), policy hooks (THE GATE TRAVELS), and identity (toolfunnel.json, rewritten with the pack's name/version; a declared `requires` travels too) - into <home>/dist/<out||name>/, a SEPARATE location from the live config. format 'home' = a portable config home (zip / git init / run via --config-dir). format 'npm' = a publishable package: generated package.json with toolfunnel as a caret DEPENDENCY (depend, never copy), a 2-line bin launcher pointing --config-dir at the bundled home/, and a README stub - then `cd dist/<name> && npm publish`. NEVER ships auth/ or logs/. Refuses a non-empty destination without force:true. out is a folder NAME under dist/, never a path.

format.description: Artifact type: portable config home (default) or publishable npm package.

version.description: Pack version (default: the home's serverVersion, else 0.1.0).

force.description: Overwrite files in an existing non-empty destination.
